22	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
23	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
24	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
25	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
26	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
27		Log             io.Writer
28		CleanupLog      io.Writer
29		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
30		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
31		CleanupInterval time.Duration
32		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
33		EventSink       io.Writer
34		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
35		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
36		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
37		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
38		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
39		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
40		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
41		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
42		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
43		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
44		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
45		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
46		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
47		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
48		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
49		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
50		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
51		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
52		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
53		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
54		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
55		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
56		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
57		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
58		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
59		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
60		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
61		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
62		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
63		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
64		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
65		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
66		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
67		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
68		Mode         executeloop.Mode
69		IdleInterval time.Duration
70		Once         bool
71		PollInterval time.Duration
72		NoReview     bool
73		LabelFilter  string
74		SessionID    string
75		WorkerID     string
76		ProjectRoot  string
77		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
78		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
79		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
80		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
81		TargetBeadID string
82		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
83		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
84		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
85		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
86		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
87		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
88		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
89	}

91	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
92		mode := r.Mode
93		if mode == "" {
94			if r.Once {
95				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
96			} else {
97				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
98			}
99		}
100		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
101		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
102			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
103		}
104		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
105			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
106		}
107		return mode, idleInterval
108	}

110	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
111	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
112	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
113	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
114	
115	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
116	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
117	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
118	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
119	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
120	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
121	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
122	
123	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
124	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
125	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
126	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
127	// advance the base branch.
128	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
129	
130	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
131	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
132	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
133	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
134	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
135	// worker.
136	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
137	
138	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
139	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
140	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
141	// triage:needs-investigation park.
142	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute

144	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
145	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
146	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
147	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
148	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
149	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
150	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
151	//
152	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
153	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
154	//
155	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
156	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
157	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
158	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
159	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
160	// directly.
161	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
162		ctx context.Context,
163		projectRoot string,
164		b *bead.Bead,
165		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
166		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
167		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
168		actor, source string,
169		now func() time.Time,
170	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
171		if now == nil {
172			now = time.Now
173		}
174		if submit == nil {
175			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
176		}
177		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
178		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
179		if err != nil {
180			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
181			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
182			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
183			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
184			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
185				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
186			}
187			return &LandResult{
188				Status:      "preserved",
189				PreserveRef: ref,
190				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
191			}, nil, nil
192		}
193		if outcome.Blocked {
194			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
195			if perr != nil {
196				return nil, outcome, perr
197			}
198			if eventStore != nil {
199				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
200			}
201			return land, outcome, nil
202		}
203		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
204			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
205		}
206		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
207		return land, outcome, sErr
208	}

210	// handlePostAttemptDecomposition runs the orchestrator-level splitter when a
211	// no_changes attempt signals orchestrator_action: decompose. It checks the
212	// queue-level max_decomposition_depth (not the implementation prompt cap),
213	// validates the AC map for completeness, and either creates children+deps or
214	// parks the parent for operator review if the split is lossy or depth-capped.
215	// The bead must already be unclaimed before this is called.
216	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handlePostAttemptDecomposition(ctx context.Context, candidate *bead.Bead, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, at time.Time) {
217		emit := func(kind string, body map[string]any) {
218			if runtime.EventSink == nil {
219				return
220			}
221			data, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{"event": kind, "payload": body})
222			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.EventSink, "%s\n", data)
223		}
224		parkOperator := func(reason string) {
225			if runtime.Log != nil {
226				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition blocked: %s (%s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
227			}
228			emit("post_attempt_decomposition.blocked", map[string]any{
229				"bead_id": candidate.ID,
230				"reason":  reason,
231			})
232			if berr := parkBeadPostIntakeRejection(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, reason, at); berr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
233				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition park error: %v\n", berr)
234			}
235		}
236	
237		// Queue-level depth check: orchestrator uses its own cap, not the
238		// implementation prompt's hardcoded depth-2 cap.
239		maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()
240		if maxDepth > 0 && storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate) >= maxDepth {
241			overflowBody, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
242				"depth": storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate),
243				"max":   maxDepth,
244			})
245			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
246				Kind:      "triage-overflow",
247				Summary:   "depth cap exceeded during post-attempt decomposition",
248				Body:      string(overflowBody),
249				Actor:     assignee,
250				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
251				CreatedAt: at,
252			})
253			parkOperator("queue-level depth cap exceeded; operator must split")
254			return
255		}
256	
257		decomp, err := runtime.PostAttemptDecompositionHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
258		if err != nil {
259			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition hook error: %s", err.Error()))
260			return
261		}
262		if decomp == nil {
263			parkOperator("decomposition hook returned no split")
264			return
265		}
266		lossyOrEmpty := isDecompositionLossy(decomp.ACMap) || (len(decomp.ACMap) == 0 && strings.TrimSpace(candidate.Acceptance) != "")
267		if lossyOrEmpty {
268			parkOperator("decomposition AC map is incomplete; operator must produce a lossless split")
269			return
270		}
271	
272		childIDs, decompErr := applyPreClaimDecomposition(w.Store, candidate, decomp, assignee, at)
273		if decompErr != nil {
274			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition apply error: %s", decompErr.Error()))
275			return
276		}
277		if runtime.Log != nil {
278			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition: bead %s split into %s\n", candidate.ID, strings.Join(childIDs, ", "))
279		}
280		emit("post_attempt_decomposition.applied", map[string]any{
281			"bead_id":   candidate.ID,
282			"child_ids": childIDs,
283		})
284	}

286	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
287	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
288	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
289	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
290	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
291		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
292		if hook == nil {
293			return report
294		}
295		if isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(report.OutcomeReason) {
296			return report
297		}
298		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
299		if err != nil {

300		if err != nil {
301			if runtime.Log != nil {
302				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
303			}
304			return report
305		}
306		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
307		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
308		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
309		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
310			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
311		}
312		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
313			return report
314		}
315		if classificationRecognized {
316			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
317		}
318		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
319		return report
320	}

322	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
323	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
324	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
325	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
326		if d <= 0 {
327			return 0
328		}
329		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
330			return MaxLoopCooldown
331		}
332		return d
333	}

// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "work",
		Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
		Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.

ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
bounds.

Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
label; otherwise the label is ignored.

Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
  drained     — no ready beads remain
  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts

work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
`,
		Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
  ddx work

  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
  ddx work --once

  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
  ddx work --watch

  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s

  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7

  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing

  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
		RunE: f.runWork,
	}

	// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
	// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
	cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
	cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
	cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
	cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
	cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
	cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
	cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
	_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
	cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
	cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
	cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
	cmd.Flags().Float64("max-bead-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxBeadCostUSD, "Per-bead cost budget in USD; stop escalating when this bead's billed cost exceeds this amount (0 = unlimited); overridden per-bead by a budget:<USD> label")
	cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
	// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
	cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
		"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
	// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope

	cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
	cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")

15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}

## Codebase Structure Analysis for Metrics Extraction Feature

Based on my exploration of the DDx codebase, here's the comprehensive overview for implementing a metrics extraction feature:

### 1. Bead Attempt Finalization Flow

**Key File:** `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`

The finalization happens through the `ExecuteBeadReport` struct (lines 335-431), which captures:
- **Execution metrics**: `Status`, `Detail`, `Error`, `Stderr`
- **Routing data**: `Harness`, `Provider`, `Model`, `ActualPower`, `PredictedPower`, `PredictedSpeedTPS`, `PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens`
- **Git state**: `BaseRev`, `ResultRev`, `ImplementationRev`, `LandedRev`, `EvidenceRev`
- **Timing**: All costs and performance metrics are computed before report is finalized
- **Review data**: `ReviewVerdict`, `ReviewRationale`
- **Outcome classification**: `OutcomeReason`, `Disrupted`, `DisruptionReason`

**Finalization Points:**
- Line 1334, 1400, 1472, 1847: `executeBeadLoopEvent()` creates standardized bead events
- Line 62-94 in `execute_bead_report.go`: `ReportFromExecuteBeadResult()` maps detailed results to loop-facing reports

### 2. Event Schema (events.jsonl)

**File Example:** `.ddx/attachments/*/events.jsonl`

Each event is a single-line JSON with this structure:
```json
{
  "actor": "ddx|erik",
  "body": "{...JSON body...}",
  "created_at": "ISO8601 timestamp",
  "kind": "routing|execute-bead|review-malfunction|etc",
  "source": "ddx agent execute-bead|ddx agent execute-loop",
  "summary": "provider=claude|task_succeeded|BLOCK without rationale"
}
```

**Key Event Kinds:**
- `routing` — provider/model resolution (body: `resolved_provider`, `resolved_model`, `fallback_chain`, `requested_profile`, `requested_tier`, `resolved_tier`, `escalation_count`, `final_tier`)
- `execute-bead` — attempt outcome with full summary (body: multi-line key=value pairs including status, detail, tier, probe_result, rationale, result_rev, base_rev, retry_after, outcome_reason, predicted metrics)
- `review-malfunction` — review verdict issues

### 3. ExecuteBeadResult Structure

**File:** `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` (lines 25-126)

Complete attempt metadata:
```go
type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
    BeadID, AttemptID, WorkerID string
    BaseRev, ResultRev string                    // git state
    ImplementationRev, LandedRev, EvidenceRev string
    Outcome, Status, OrchestratorStatus string  // lifecycle
    Harness, Provider, Model string             // routing
    ActualPower int                             // routing decision power
    DurationMS int                              // wall-clock time
    Tokens int                                  // token usage
    CostUSD float64                             // billed cost
    ExitCode int                                // process exit
    FailureMode string                          // classification
    ExecutionDir string                         // evidence directory
    ManifestFile, ResultFile, PromptFile string // artifact paths
    AttemptDiagnostics *AttemptDiagnostic      // fallback diagnostics
}
```

**Files Generated Per Attempt:**
- `manifest.json` — harness/model/version metadata (in ExecutionDir)
- `result.json` — complete ExecuteBeadResult (in ExecutionDir)
- `prompt.md` — operator-facing prompt
- Evidence directory at: `.ddx/executions/{bead-id}/{attempt-id}/`

### 4. CLI Command Pattern

**File:** `/cli/cmd/work.go` (lines 1-134)

Standard Cobra command structure:
```go
func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
    cmd := &cobra.Command{
        Use: "work",
        Short: "...",
        Long: "...",
        Example: "...",
        RunE: f.runWork,  // execution handler
    }
    cmd.Flags().String("flag-name", "default", "description")
    // ... more flags
    return cmd
}

func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    flagValue, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("flag-name")
    // implementation
}
```

**Key Work Flags:**
- `--project` — target project root
- `--harness`, `--model`, `--provider` — passthrough routing constraints
- `--once` — execute single bead and stop
- `--watch` — keep polling after queue drains
- `--no-review` — skip post-merge review
- `--json` — output as JSON

### 5. Agent-Facing Report Finalization

**Line 2271-2330 (executeBeadLoopEvent):**

The event body uses multi-line key=value format:
```
status
detail (if present)
tier=value (if present)
probe_result=value (if present)
rationale: text (if present)
review_rationale (if present)
preserve_ref=sha
result_rev=sha
base_rev=sha
retry_after=duration
outcome_reason=classification
predicted_power=int
predicted_speed_tps=float
predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens=float source=source_name
```

### 6. Loop Event Emission

**Lines 2362-2381 (writeLoopEvent):**

Structured event envelope for EventSink (io.Writer):
```json
{
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "type": "event_type",
  "ts": "RFC3339Nano timestamp",
  "data": { /* structured data */ }
}
```

Emitted during:
- Queue state changes
- Resource preflight checks
- Decomposition decisions
- Post-attempt triage

### 7. Bead Store Interface

**Lines 443-466 (ExecuteBeadLoopStore):**

Key finalization operations:
- `AppendEvent(id, event)` — records BeadEvent on bead (used for execute-bead events)
- `CloseWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA)` — marks bead complete
- `SetExecutionCooldown(id, until, status, detail, baseRev)` — parks with retry schedule
- `Unclaim(id)` — releases claim when attempt completes

---

## Implementation Points for Metrics Extraction

1. **Read ExecuteBeadReport** during finalization (line 1334+) to capture metrics before they're discarded
2. **Access events.jsonl** in `.ddx/attachments/{worktree-id}/` directory
3. **Parse JSON event bodies** for routing/provider metadata and attempt metrics
4. **Emit structured events** via `writeLoopEvent()` into EventSink for external consumption
5. **Query ExecuteBeadLoopResult** (lines 542-554) for aggregate loop metrics: attempts, successes, failures, stop condition
6. **Read result.json** from ExecutionDir for complete attempt metadata including predictive routing estimates

**Key Files to Reference:**
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — Main loop orchestration (3500+ lines)
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — Result data structures
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report.go` — Report finalization
- `/cli/cmd/work.go` — CLI entry point pattern
- `/cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go` — Single-bead executor pattern

29	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
30	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
31	// returned to a caller.

44		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
45		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`

47		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.

50		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
51		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
52		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /

3	import (

9	)

11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68	
69		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
70		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
71		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
72		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
74		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
76		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
77		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
78		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
81		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
82		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
84		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
87		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
89		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
90		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-bead-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxBeadCostUSD, "Per-bead cost budget in USD; stop escalating when this bead's billed cost exceeds this amount (0 = unlimited); overridden per-bead by a budget:<USD> label")
91		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
92		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
93		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
94			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
95		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
96		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
97		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
99	
100		// Register "ddx work plan", "ddx work focus", and "ddx work clear-cooldowns" as subcommands.
101		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
102		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
103		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkClearCooldownsCommand())
104	
105		return cmd
106	}
107	
108	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
109	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
110	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
111	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
112	// by this layer.
113	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
114		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
115			return err
116		}
117	
118		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
119		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
120	
121		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
122		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
123		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
124		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
125		if !asJSON {
126			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
127			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
128				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
129			})
130		}
131	
132		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
133	}

27	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
28		Log             io.Writer
29		CleanupLog      io.Writer
30		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
31		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
32		CleanupInterval time.Duration
33		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
34		EventSink       io.Writer
35		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
36		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
37		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
38		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
39		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
40		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
41		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
42		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
43		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
44		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
45		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
46		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
47		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
48		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
49		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
50		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
51		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
52		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
53		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
54		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
55		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
56		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
57		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
58		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
59		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
60		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
61		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
62		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
63		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
64		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
65		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
66		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
67		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
68		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
69		Mode         executeloop.Mode
70		IdleInterval time.Duration
71		Once         bool
72		PollInterval time.Duration
73		NoReview     bool
74		LabelFilter  string
75		SessionID    string
76		WorkerID     string
77		ProjectRoot  string
78		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
79		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
80		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
81		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
82		TargetBeadID string
83		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
84		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
85		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
86		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
87		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
88		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
89		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
90	}
91	
92	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
93		mode := r.Mode
94		if mode == "" {
95			if r.Once {
96				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
97			} else {
98				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
99			}
100		}
101		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
102		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
103			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
104		}
105		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
106			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
107		}
108		return mode, idleInterval
109	}
110	
111	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
112	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
113	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
114	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
115	
116	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
117	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
118	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
119	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
120	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
121	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
122	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
123	
124	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
125	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
126	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
127	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
128	// advance the base branch.
129	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
130	
131	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
132	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
133	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
134	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
135	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
136	// worker.

21	// CompareRuntime carrying per-arm harness/model/label data plus the
22	// caller-provided base AgentRunRuntime (Prompt, PromptFile, WorkDir, ...).
23	func BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms []BenchmarkArm, base AgentRunRuntime) CompareRuntime {
24		harnesses := make([]string, len(arms))
25		armModels := make(map[int]string, len(arms))
26		armLabels := make(map[int]string, len(arms))

33			}

35		}
36

38			AgentRunRuntime: base,
39			Harnesses:       harnesses,
40			ArmModels:       armModels,
41			ArmLabels:       armLabels,

1487	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd *cobra.Command, treatPassthroughAsOpaque bool, tryTargetBeadID string) error {
1488		spec, dispatch, err := parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd, treatPassthroughAsOpaque)
1489		if err != nil {
1490			return err
1491		}
1492		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(spec.ProjectRoot, f.WorkingDir)
1493		spec.ProjectRoot = projectRoot
1494		if tryTargetBeadID != "" {
1495			// `ddx try <bead-id>` is a single-bead one-shot: target one bead and
1496			// exit after one attempt regardless of CLI flag state.
1497			spec.Mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
1498			spec.IdleInterval = executeloop.Duration{}
1499		}
1500	
1501		// Zero-config auto-route: when the operator passes no routing flags and
1502		// the project has no .ddx/config.yaml routing block, dispatch flows
1503		// through fizeau's provider resolution. Provider configuration and any
1504		// "no providers configured" error reporting is fizeau's concern; ddx
1505		// does not read fizeau's config files.
1506		// ADR-022: queue work always runs inline. The legacy --local flag is
1507		// a deprecated no-op and the server-submit path is removed from this CLI
1508		// surface — server-spawned workers exec `ddx work` directly.
1509	
1510		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1511		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1512		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1513		// any bead status changes hands.
1514		// Skipped for ddx work (treatPassthroughAsOpaque=true): harness/provider/model
1515		// are opaque passthrough constraints there; DDx does not validate them.
1516		if !spec.OpaquePassthrough {
1517			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, spec.Harness, spec.Model, spec.Provider, spec.ModelRef); err != nil {
1518				return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
1519			}
1520		}
1521	
1522		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1523	
1524		// Structured progress sink for this loop run. Events emitted at
1525		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end land here so
1526		// log aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, `ddx server workers log`)
1527		// can parse the same JSONL envelope used by harness session logs.
1528		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
1529		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
1530		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)

package agent

import (

func TestMetrics_SchemaVersion(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	store := NewAttemptMetricsStore(dir)
	row := metricsRowFromReport(ExecuteBeadReport{
		BeadID:    "ddx-test",
		AttemptID: "20260101T000000-aabbccdd",
		Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
	}, time.Now().UTC())
	require.NoError(t, store.AppendRow(row))

	rows, err := store.ReadRows()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, rows, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, AttemptMetricsSchemaVersion, rows[0].SchemaVersion)
}

func TestMetrics_AppendRowOnFinalization(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	store := NewAttemptMetricsStore(dir)

	tsEnd := time.Date(2026, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	report := ExecuteBeadReport{
		BeadID:          "ddx-abc123",
		AttemptID:       "20260101T120000-aabbccdd",
		SessionID:       "sess-1",
		Model:           "claude-sonnet-4-6",
		Harness:         "claude",
		Provider:        "anthropic",
		Status:          ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
		CostUSD:         1.23,
		DurationMS:      5000,
		ReviewVerdict:   "APPROVE",
		PredictedPower:  70,
		ActualPower:     70,
		EscalationCount: 0,
	}

	row := metricsRowFromReport(report, tsEnd)
	require.NoError(t, store.AppendRow(row))

	rows, err := store.ReadRows()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, rows, 1)

	r := rows[0]
	assert.Equal(t, AttemptMetricsSchemaVersion, r.SchemaVersion)
	assert.Equal(t, "ddx-abc123", r.BeadID)
	assert.Equal(t, "20260101T120000-aabbccdd", r.AttemptID)
	assert.Equal(t, "sess-1", r.SessionID)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude-sonnet-4-6", r.Model)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude", r.Harness)
	assert.Equal(t, "anthropic", r.Provider)
	assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, r.Outcome)
	assert.InDelta(t, 1.23, r.CostUSD, 0.001)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(5000), r.DurationMS)
	assert.Equal(t, "APPROVE", r.ReviewVerdict)
	assert.Equal(t, 70, r.LadderMinPowerInitial)
	assert.Equal(t, 70, r.LadderMinPowerFinal)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, r.LadderStepsTaken)
	assert.Equal(t, tsEnd, r.TsEnd)
	assert.Equal(t, tsEnd.Add(-5*time.Second), r.TsStart)

	// Verify the raw file is JSONL with schema_version.
	p := filepath.Join(dir, attemptMetricsDir, attemptMetricsFile)
	data, err := os.ReadFile(p)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var raw map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(data, &raw))
	sv, ok := raw["schema_version"]
	require.True(t, ok, "schema_version key missing from raw JSONL")
	assert.Equal(t, float64(AttemptMetricsSchemaVersion), sv)
}

func TestMetrics_BackfillFromEvents(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()

	// Build a synthetic events.jsonl for bead "ddx-b1".
	beadID := "ddx-b1"
	attachDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx", "attachments", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(attachDir, 0o755))

	tsRef := time.Date(2026, 1, 2, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	costBody, _ := json.Marshal(costEventBody{
		AttemptID:    "20260102T100000-cafebabe",
		Harness:      "claude",
		Provider:     "anthropic",
		Model:        "claude-sonnet-4-6",
		InputTokens:  100,
		OutputTokens: 200,
		TotalTokens:  300,
		CostUSD:      0.5,
		DurationMS:   3000,
		ExitCode:     0,
	})

	writeEvent := func(f *os.File, kind, summary, body, source string, ts time.Time) {
		ev := beadEventForBackfill{
			Kind:      kind,
			Summary:   summary,
			Body:      body,
			Source:    source,
			CreatedAt: ts,
		}
		line, _ := json.Marshal(ev)
		_, _ = f.Write(append(line, '\n'))
	}

	evFile := filepath.Join(attachDir, "events.jsonl")
	f, err := os.Create(evFile)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	writeEvent(f, "cost", "tokens=300 cost_usd=0.5000", string(costBody), "ddx agent execute-bead", tsRef)
	writeEvent(f, "review", "APPROVE", "", "ddx agent execute-loop", tsRef.Add(time.Second))
	writeEvent(f, "execute-bead", ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, "", "ddx agent execute-loop", tsRef.Add(2*time.Second))
	f.Close()

	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, added)

	rows, err := store.ReadRows()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, rows, 1)

	r := rows[0]
	assert.Equal(t, AttemptMetricsSchemaVersion, r.SchemaVersion)
	assert.Equal(t, "20260102T100000-cafebabe", r.AttemptID)
	assert.Equal(t, beadID, r.BeadID)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude", r.Harness)
	assert.Equal(t, "anthropic", r.Provider)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude-sonnet-4-6", r.Model)
	assert.Equal(t, 100, r.InputTokens)
	assert.Equal(t, 200, r.OutputTokens)
	assert.Equal(t, 300, r.TotalTokens)
	assert.InDelta(t, 0.5, r.CostUSD, 0.001)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(3000), r.DurationMS)
	assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, r.Outcome)
	assert.Equal(t, "APPROVE", r.ReviewVerdict)
}

func TestMetrics_BackfillIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()

	beadID := "ddx-idem"
	attachDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx", "attachments", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(attachDir, 0o755))

	tsRef := time.Date(2026, 1, 3, 8, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	costBody, _ := json.Marshal(costEventBody{
		AttemptID: "20260103T080000-deadbeef",
		Harness:   "agent",
		CostUSD:   0.1,
		DurationMS: 1000,
	})

	evFile := filepath.Join(attachDir, "events.jsonl")
	f, err := os.Create(evFile)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	ev := beadEventForBackfill{Kind: "cost", Body: string(costBody), CreatedAt: tsRef}
	line, _ := json.Marshal(ev)
	_, _ = f.Write(append(line, '\n'))
	ev2 := beadEventForBackfill{Kind: "execute-bead", Summary: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, CreatedAt: tsRef.Add(time.Second)}
	line2, _ := json.Marshal(ev2)
	_, _ = f.Write(append(line2, '\n'))
	f.Close()

	// First run: adds one row.
	added1, err := BackfillAttemptMetrics(dir)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, added1)

	// Second run: same data, no duplicates.
	added2, err := BackfillAttemptMetrics(dir)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, added2)

	// File still has exactly one row.
	store := NewAttemptMetricsStore(dir)
	rows, err := store.ReadRows()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Len(t, rows, 1)
}
